name: Cabarrus County Schools
districtFact: Cabarrus County Schools serves families in the Charlotte metro, including Concord, Harrisburg, Midland, Mount Pleasant, and other fast-growing communities northeast of Mecklenburg County.

about.title: Which CCS Calendar Should You Use?
about.content: Most students use the Cabarrus County Schools Academic Calendar. CCS also publishes a separate Early College calendar and a Restart calendar for Concord Middle School, Rocky River Elementary, Royal Oaks, W.M. Irvin Elementary, and Winecoff Elementary. Confirm the student's school or program before relying on first day, exam, early dismissal, and break dates.

planningTips.title: Planning Tips for Cabarrus County Families
content: Download or sync the CCS calendar before booking travel, camps, or recurring childcare.
Confirm whether your student follows the traditional, Early College, or Restart calendar.
Add capped workdays, uncapped workdays, and early dismissal days to family calendars.
Compare CCS and CMS break dates before booking Charlotte-area flights or shared childcare.
Verify school assignment by exact address before moving to Concord, Harrisburg, Midland, Mount Pleasant, or Kannapolis-area neighborhoods.
Check school-level exam, athletics, graduation, and transportation updates separately.

compareIntro: Cabarrus County Schools families most often compare CCS with Charlotte-Mecklenburg Schools and Union County Public Schools because these districts affect nearby childcare, shared custody, youth sports, and Charlotte-area travel planning.

districtFaqs.q: Which Cabarrus County Schools calendar should families use?
districtFaqs.a: Most students follow the CCS Academic Calendar. Students in Early College programs should use the Early College calendar, and students at Concord Middle, Rocky River Elementary, Royal Oaks, W.M. Irvin Elementary, or Winecoff Elementary should check the Restart calendar.

districtFaqs.q: What are capped and uncapped workdays in Cabarrus County Schools?
districtFaqs.a: For families, capped and uncapped workdays usually mean students do not attend school. The distinction affects staff reporting and calendar operations, but parents should treat both as no-school weekdays when planning childcare.

districtFaqs.q: Does CCS have early dismissal days?
districtFaqs.a: Yes. CCS lists early dismissal dates tied to end-of-quarter days and high school exam periods. Restart schools may also have restart-specific early dismissal dates.

districtFaqs.q: How does CCS handle inclement weather?
districtFaqs.a: The official CCS calendar notes that North Carolina requires missed instructional time to be made up to meet the 1,025-hour requirement. The superintendent may waive banked hours, and up to 30 hours may be used for remote learning due to inclement weather.

districtFaqs.q: Can CCS calendar dates change after approval?
districtFaqs.a: Yes. The 2025-2026 calendar was revised after approval, and districts can update calendars for board action, weather, state requirements, or operational needs. Families should re-check the official CCS calendar page before booking nonrefundable travel.

districtFaqs.q: Do CCS high schools have different graduation dates?
districtFaqs.a: Yes. The CCS academic calendar gives the graduation window, but individual high school ceremonies are scheduled separately. Seniors should follow school-level announcements for exact graduation times and venues.

districtFaqs.q: Does CCS Early College follow the same calendar?
districtFaqs.a: No. CCS publishes a separate Early College calendar. Early College families should use that PDF for first day, breaks, workdays, and program-specific planning rather than relying only on the traditional academic calendar.

livingHere.intro: Cabarrus County sits on the northeast side of the Charlotte metro. Families often choose the area for Concord-area housing, Harrisburg access to Charlotte, motorsports and I-85 employment corridors, or a less urban school district than CMS while staying close to the region's job market.

highlights.label: Concord and I-85 Commuting
highlights.detail: Families in Concord often balance CCS school schedules with I-85 commuting, motorsports-area employment, and Charlotte or University City workdays. Early dismissal and exam days can change pickup routines.

highlights.label: Harrisburg and Mecklenburg County Border
highlights.detail: Families in Harrisburg often compare CCS with CMS because the communities are close geographically, but the school districts, break dates, and calendar decisions are separate.

highlights.label: Midland and Mount Pleasant Planning
highlights.detail: Families in Midland and Mount Pleasant should check transportation, school assignment, and program calendars carefully because rural and suburban commute patterns can make early dismissal days harder to cover.

highlights.label: Restart Schools
highlights.detail: Concord Middle School, Rocky River Elementary, Royal Oaks, W.M. Irvin Elementary, and Winecoff Elementary have a Restart calendar. Families at those schools should not rely only on the traditional district calendar.

highlights.label: Weather and Remote Learning
highlights.detail: Cabarrus County can see winter ice and severe storms. CCS notes that banked hours and remote learning hours may be used after inclement weather.

customSections.label: Verification and Editorial Process
customSections.content: MySchoolDates is an independent calendar reference and is not affiliated with Cabarrus County Schools. This page is built from the official CCS calendar page and the district's published academic calendar PDFs. Reviewed July 15, 2026 against the 2025-2026 revised traditional PDF, the 2026-2027 traditional PDF approved October 13, 2025, the Early College PDFs, and the Restart calendar PDFs. The review checked first day, last day, major breaks, holidays, student no-school workdays, early dismissal dates, exam windows, graduation-window notes, alternate-calendar links, and calendar-app export behavior. Staff-only labels are translated into family-facing no-school dates only when they affect students.

relatedDistricts.name: Charlotte-Mecklenburg Schools
relatedDistricts.comparisonNote: Neighboring Mecklenburg County district. Families in Harrisburg and Concord often compare CCS and CMS calendars, commutes, and school assignment rules.

relatedDistricts.name: Union County Public Schools
relatedDistricts.comparisonNote: Another fast-growing Charlotte suburban district, useful for comparing spring break, workdays, and suburban school assignment patterns.

sources.label: North Carolina Department of Public Instruction
